OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

Being an electrician in New York City keeps me busy with work, but it doesn't stop people from bringing this up during coffee breaks or while volunteering at local community events.\n\nI'm married with one child, and I've got moderate depression which makes these talks harder for me personally. And with my low extraversion score? response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As someone who's been through quite a bit, I've learned that managing conversations on sensitive topics like COVID vaccines takes patience. You got to listen more than you talk, especially when people are emotional or misinformed. Being an electrician in New York City keeps me busy with work, but it doesn't stop people from bringing this up during coffee breaks or while volunteering at local community events.

I'm married with one child, and I've got moderate depression which makes these talks harder for me personally. But my Hindu beliefs teach compassion and truthfulness so when disagreements arise about vaccines—say someone thinks they're unsafe—I try to stick to facts without being confrontational because agreeableness is key here.

People often say stuff online but since I'm only on social media a few times weekly, most conversations happen face-to-face at work or around town. And with my low extraversion score? I prefer one-on-one chats over big crowds anyway.

What helps me manage this stress (which is high because of conscientiousness) is sticking to reliable sources like CDC and WHO reports while dealing with misinformation spread by people who don't understand how these things work—especially those quoting older studies without updates.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Starting from personal experience managing vaccine discussions in a busy environment, I considered my low extraversion personality trait that makes group debates uncomfortable. My high agreeableness and moderate depression complicate emotional responses while Hinduism provides a moral framework for handling conflict through truthfulness and compassion.
response_parsed.tone: Calm but cautious due to moderate neuroticism score
